title: [CI:DOCS] Update container image docs + fix unstable execution
body:
Update the order of image documentation to be from most to least stable.
Similarly, avoid depending on execution of upstream podman, when
building/pushing.  It's easily possible for this build to function but
execution to fail due to some partially implemented feature.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL, this changes behavior to favor pushing a possibly non-functional 'upstream' podman image vs not pushing anything ([example failure](https://github.com/containers/podman/runs/2445972598?check_suite_focus=true#step:7:53)).  Since nobody's watching these daily builds, I figured it was better to have builds/pushes happening than potentially hosting a stale 'upstream' image for indefinite time periods.

@TomSweeneyRedHat sorry, it just occurred to me: For stable, we never push a `latest` tag, only the versioned tag (if it doesn't exist).  Fix this can be done but it's a bit tricky, I'll need to do some refactoring...
...done.  I also tweaked the docs to show we always push a 'latest' tag for all images.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
[Oof.  Have I mentioned how much I dislike working with github-actions?](https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/798759907)
